text:
Once upon a time, there was a little rabbit named Little Flop. Little Flop loved to hop around in the meadows, eating mint leaves and looking for bugs. One sunny morning, Little Flop stumbled upon a mysterious old owl perched on an oak tree.

The owl blinked in surprise at Little Flop, then chuckled, "Fear not, dear little hoppy friend! I haven'etched to scare you."

Little Flop curiously approached the owl. It turned out the owl could speak rabbit-elegant fluently. Together they discussed how larks sing sweet melodies at dawn, but nobody knows why owls laugh so loud at night. 

Little Flop had a marvelous idea. "Why don't we hold a singing owl night? You tell funny owl-limericks, and I'll sing melodies."

The owl loved that suggestion. They gathered a group of birds, rabbits, and even some squirrels who enjoyed a good laugh. Under the moonlight, they filled
